Title: Interactions of separately conserved α-(1→6) glucosidases that participate in maize endosperm starch biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W64A; Ia453; heterologous yeast strains 808 and 830
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pullulanase-type debranching enzyme; CAZy GH13_13 α-(1→6)-glucosidase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes α-(1→6) branch linkages in α-polyglucans such as pullulan and amylopectin
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Debranched α-glucans; released branch-point cleavage products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon storage reserve in maize endosperm and determinant of kernel quality
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid/amyloplast (inferred from starch biosynthetic context and transit peptide discussion)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing kernels harvested 20 days after pollination; heterologous galactose-induced expression in yeast
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for proper glucan remodeling that promotes starch crystallization and limits phytoglycogen accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Activity in Red Pullulan assays was stated to be rate-limiting in endosperm extracts; key contributor to debranching capacity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes normal starch accumulation and kernel phenotype; reduced activity associates with increased phytoglycogen and low-starch sugary phenotype; restored/elevated activity associates with suppression of su1-Ref phytoglycogen accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Null mutant analysis (zpu1-204); enzyme activity assays in maize endosperm and engineered yeast; immunoblotting; co-immunoprecipitation; yeast two-hybrid; suppressor-line metabolite/phenotype association
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. zpu1-204 reduced Red Pullulan hydrolysis to ~5% of wild type, showing ZPU1 provides most detectable pullulanase activity in maize endosperm. 2. ZPU1 activity was reduced in isa1/su1 and isa2 mutant backgrounds, and increased ~1.7-fold when ISA1 was coexpressed in yeast. 3. ZPU1 directly interacts with ISA1 and occurs with ISA1 in native complexes. 4. Restoration of ZPU1 activity in su1-Ref suppressor lines correlated with recovery of near-normal starch and low phytoglycogen.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to improve endosperm starch quality and reduce phytoglycogen in sugary backgrounds; likely most effective when engineered with ISA1/ISA2-compatible context rather than alone
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering ZPU1 alone may be insufficient because its activity depends on ISA1/ISA2 context; possible shifts between starch and phytoglycogen
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Two SDS-PAGE mobility forms were observed; ISA1 state altered ZPU1 mobility and abundance. Direct ZPU1-ISA2 interaction was not detected in yeast, but ISA2 affected ZPU1 activity indirectly.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W64A; Ia453; P39; A632; heterologous yeast strains 806 and 830
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Isoamylase-type debranching enzyme; CAZy GH13_11 α-(1→6)-glucosidase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes α-(1→6) branch linkages in amylopectin-like glucans but not pullulan
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Debranched glucan chains conducive to starch crystallization
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon storage reserve in maize endosperm and determinant of kernel quality
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing kernels harvested 20 DAP; heterologous galactose-induced expression in yeast
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for proper glucan trimming that enables starch accumulation and prevents phytoglycogen formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control enzyme for normal starch versus phytoglycogen partitioning in maize endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss or catalytic impairment causes classic sugary phenotype with low starch and high phytoglycogen; also decreases ZPU1 activity and alters ZPU1 mobility/abundance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant allele series in maize (su1-Ref, su1-st, su1-am, su1-Bn2, su1-NC, su1-4582); enzyme zymograms; Red Pullulan assays via effect on ZPU1; yeast coexpression assays; yeast two-hybrid; co-immunoprecipitation; suppressor-line analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ISA1 is the su1 gene product and is essential for normal endosperm starch accumulation. 2. Multiple isa1/su1 alleles reduced ZPU1 activity, while ISA1 coexpression stimulated ZPU1 activity in yeast. 3. ISA1 binds itself, ISA2, and ZPU1. 4. su1-Ref suppressor lines restored starch accumulation without restoring ISA1 activity, indicating partial functional bypass via other components including ZPU1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central target for controlling sugary versus starchy endosperm traits; engineering should consider interaction with ZPU1 and ISA2 and possible bypass suppressors
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes severe phytoglycogen accumulation and low-starch kernels; some alleles reduce partner-protein abundance or alter mobility
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: su1-Ref W578R prevents ISA complex activity; suppressor lines remained su1-Ref homozygotes yet recovered near-normal starch, implying extragenic suppression.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Isoamylase-type partner protein; CAZy GH13_11 family component of ISA1/ISA2 heteromultimer
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as part of ISA1/ISA2 heteromultimeric debranching complexes influencing α-(1→6)-glucan hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major carbon storage reserve in maize endosperm and determinant of kernel quality
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supports ISA1/ISA2 complex function required for normal starch accumulation and indirectly supports ZPU1 activity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key complex component, but not independently established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: isa2-339 reduced ZPU1 activity by ~25–50%, indicating ISA2 contributes to functional complexes supporting starch biosynthesis and limiting phytoglycogen
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Null mutant analysis (isa2-339); Red Pullulan assays in maize endosperm; yeast two-hybrid interaction assays; immunoblot analysis in suppressor lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ISA2 forms heteromultimeric complexes with ISA1 and also shows self-interaction in yeast. 2. Deletion of ISA2 reduced ZPU1 activity in maize endosperm even though ISA1 homodimer remains. 3. No direct ISA2-ZPU1 interaction was detected in yeast, suggesting indirect influence through ISA1-containing complexes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant companion target when optimizing starch structure and debranching efficiency; should be considered jointly with ISA1 and ZPU1
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Removing ISA2 lowers ZPU1 activity and may impair starch quality despite persistence of ISA1 homodimer
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Suppressor-line sequencing indicated ISA2 coding region was unchanged, arguing ISA2 mutation is not the cause of su1-Ref suppression.
